commits.message:
terminal tokenizer: route same-line comment suffix through the parser

The same-line /* ... */ branch pushed the comment span but dropped the
suffix after the close, so a declaration trailing a same-line block comment
never flowed through tmDecl. Mirror the multi-line-close branch (which
already tokenizes the suffix) so every line routes through one parser path
and no input is lost. [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow]

Current data has no such line, but the tokenizer is a general representation
and must be honest about the whole grammar.

mit-styling-aj1.5.2: the terminal — a syntax-lit code editor that types the rule

The Pin movement's terminal node was a generic flat dark window: gray dots, a
blue prompt glyph per line, one ink color, a big void of black below. Replace it
with the movement's authoring surface — a real code EDITOR, distinct from the
runway's scroll-window and the gauge's dial:

- Tabbed window chrome (true macOS traffic-light hues) titled sticky.css, the
  .css extension wearing the section's azure.
- A line-number gutter that shares one CSS grid with the code, so numbers can
  never drift from their lines; the current line lights azure + tinted.
- Real syntax highlighting: azure properties, mint values, amber numbers, slate
  italic comments. The source is tokenised ONCE at build into {t,c} segments
  (you can't slice highlighted HTML mid-tag); the per-frame reveal walks those
  segments and block-comment state carries across lines, so the two-line CSS
  comment lights as one span. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- Char-by-char typing driven by n.w (round(progress*totalChars)) instead of
  line-by-line jumps, so the scrub uses the full resolution of paint(p,n).
- A status bar that ticks live Ln/Col and flips typing…→saved at completion.
- Window sized to its content and centred — a deliberate object, not a void.

paint(p,n) writes state only (innerHTML/textContent/class); the caret blink is a
CSS @keyframes gated by the global prefers-reduced-motion kill, not a JS branch.
The whole look lives in the terminal painter + its CSS block selected by kind;
the shared paint loop is untouched. [LAW:locality-or-seam] [LAW:decomposition]

Verified in Chrome across the scroll range: zero console errors/warnings,
clean char-by-char scrub from empty through fully-typed/saved.

mit-styling-aj1.5.1: the runway — an honest position:sticky window (#19)

* mit-styling-aj1.5.1: the runway — an honest position:sticky window

Restyle the runway node from an abstract dot-on-a-rail-beside-a-box into a
truthful position:sticky demonstration: a ribbon of page content scrolls
through a windowed offset-parent; the "sticky" band rises with the flow,
clamps to a glowing top:0 datum, holds while content slides beneath, then
releases with its section — exactly what the property does.

- The whole sticky model is four fractions (S0, SH, R, pinEnd) defined once
  in build(); ribbon height, band height and scrollbar thumb all derive from
  them, so CSS carries only the look, never the geometry. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- The three phases are a pure function of scroll (top = S0-scroll -> 0 ->
  pinEnd-scroll) — no branch on "which frame" in the paint loop. [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow]
- Live "top: Npx" readout measured from the window's real height; phase
  narration rides inside the visual. Both track raw p (a live readout), per
  the read-beat SCOPE note.
- The lock-flash WAAPI flourish is gated on REDUCE_MOTION like its siblings.

Distinct from every other section: nothing else is a windowed vertical scroll
with a pinning band (the sibling terminal is a dark code window; gauge is a dial).

Verified in Chrome across the full scroll range (in-flow / pinned / released);
zero console errors or warnings.

* runway: drive the lock-flash from CSS state, not an imperative branch

The pin-flash was fired by an if on the phase transition plus a JS
REDUCE_MOTION check — a conditional side-effect in the paint path. The
paint path already toggles .rw-datum.engaged unconditionally from the
pinned state, so move the flash to a CSS @keyframes keyed on .engaged
(fires on class-add) and let the existing global prefers-reduced-motion
motion-kill gate it, like every other CSS animation in the file.

Paint now writes state only; the phase edge-detection and the r.phase
tracking field are gone.

[LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] effect driven by state, not a branch
[LAW:single-enforcer] reduced-motion handled by the one CSS motion-kill
[LAW:one-source-of-truth] --ms-pop still the single duration, calc adds the unit
